name: Monumento Natural de los Tajos de Alhama de Granada — circular by the Embalse de Bermejales
name_original: Monumento Natural de los Tajos de Alhama de Granada — circular por el Embalse de Bermejales
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alhama De Granada traverse a landscape characterized by a deep gorge carved by the River Alhama and the impressive Tajos de Alhama canyons. The region includes parts of the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, featuring pine trees and varied mountain terrain. Cyclists will encounter rolling wheat fields, olive and almond groves, and routes around the Embalse de Bermejales reservoir. The area offers diverse routes with significant ascents and descents. Best touring cycling routes around Alhama De Granada The most popular touring cycling route is Monumento Natural de los Tajos de Alhama de Granada — circular by the Embalse de Bermejales, a 27.2 miles (43.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the dramatic Tajos de Alhama and the Embalse de Bermejales. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Below the Mirador de Alhama – Plaza de Alhama de Granada loop from Játar, a difficult 25.1 miles (40.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and views of the Alhama de Granada area. Local touring cyclists also love the Plaza de Alhama de Granada – Below the Mirador de Alhama loop from Alhama de Granada, a 19.9 miles (32.0 km) trail leading through the surroundings of Alhama de Granada, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Alhama De Granada is defined by deep gorges, expansive natural parks, and varied mountain terrain, with options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to routes around reservoirs. The routes in Alhama De Granada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alhama De Granada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alhama De Granada?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes around Alhama De Granada, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 1300 times.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The majority of touring cycling routes around Alhama De Granada are considered difficult, with 83 out of 104 routes falling into this category. These often feature significant ascents and descents due to the mountainous terrain and deep gorges. There are also 16 moderate and 5 easy routes for those seeking less challenging rides.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Alhama De Granada?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047321663/monumento-natural-de-los-tajos-de-alhama-de-granada-circular-by-the-embalse-de-bermejales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumento Natural de los Tajos de Alhama de Granada — circular by the Embalse de Bermejales&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that takes you past the dramatic Tajos de Alhama and the Embalse de Bermejales reservoir.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling around Alhama De Granada?
A: You'll experience incredibly diverse landscapes. Routes traverse deep gorges carved by the River Alhama, the impressive Tajos de Alhama canyons, and parts of the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park with its pine trees and varied mountain terrain. You'll also cycle through rolling wheat fields, olive and almond groves, and past charming small villages.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the dramatic Tajos de Alhama canyons, cycle around the scenic Embalse de Bermejales reservoir, and pass through the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. The area is also known for its thermal springs, which gave the town its name, 'Al-hama' meaning 'The Bath' in Arabic.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Alhama De Granada?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alhama De Granada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning natural scenery, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the unique experience of cycling through historic countryside and diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Sierra Nevada mountains?
A: Yes, the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, located to the south of Alhama de Granada, offers spectacular views of the Sierra Nevada mountains. Some routes within or bordering this park will provide these panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the thermal springs of Alhama De Granada?
A: While specific routes may not be dedicated solely to the thermal springs, the town of Alhama de Granada itself is famous for its curative thermal waters. Many routes start or pass through the town, allowing you to visit the free natural pools or the modern spa facility before or after your ride.

Q: What are some of the longer touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Below the Mirador de Alhama – El Boquete Viewpoint loop from Alhama de Granada&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 44.9 km, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047321663/monumento-natural-de-los-tajos-de-alhama-de-granada-circular-by-the-embalse-de-bermejales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumento Natural de los Tajos de Alhama de Granada — circular by the Embalse de Bermejales&lt;/a&gt;, at 43.8 km.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or peaks accessible from touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several stunning viewpoints and peaks. While cycling, you might encounter views of the dramatic Tajos de Alhama, the Embalse de Bermejales, and the Sierra Tejeda. For specific peaks, nearby highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/448453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maroma Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/126956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Cielo Summit&lt;/a&gt;, though reaching the very top of these might require dismounting and hiking.

Q: What kind of accommodation or rest stops can be found near the routes?
A: The area offers various options for rest and accommodation. You can find charming small villages along the routes, and there are several huts and cabins in the surrounding area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sibila Farmhouse&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/394375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Casa del Puerto 🏡 Sierra de Loja ⛰️ Zafarraya&lt;/a&gt;, which can serve as potential stops or overnight stays.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes are difficult, there are 5 easy and 16 moderate routes available. These options are generally more suitable for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous touring cycling experience. It's advisable to check the elevation profiles and distances of individual routes to find the best fit.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alhama De Granada?
A: The region offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. However, given the mountainous terrain, winters can see snow on higher peaks like Sierra Tejeda. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them ideal for touring cycling. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended.

Dungeons excavated in the same natural walls of the pits, in Alhama de Granada. Located in the oldest part of the Arab quarter of the town, the dungeons were built with a pick and shovel during the Nasrid domination, in the 13th century.

At first they were used to store and preserve grain, although during the war between the Muslim and Christian troops they were converted into prisons.

Alhama de Granada* is a picturesque town. It is situated at an altitude of around 900 metres and offers breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains and valleys. The town has a rich history dating back to Roman and Moorish times, which is reflected in its many historic buildings and monuments.
Some highlights of Alhama de Granada include:
The Arab Baths: Dating back to the 12th century, these well-preserved baths are a beautiful example of Moorish architecture.
The Iglesia Mayor de la Encarnación: Built in the 16th century, this church combines Gothic and Renaissance styles.
The Alhama Gorge: An impressive natural gorge that runs through the town and is a popular destination for walkers and nature lovers.
The Alhama Castle: A medieval fortress that offers beautiful views of the town and its surroundings.
The Arab Quarter: A preserved Moorish quarter. Alhama de Granada is famous for its thermal baths, which have been used since Roman times for their medicinal properties. The city has a relaxed and welcoming atmosphere, with narrow streets, white houses and pleasant squares where you can enjoy the local cuisine and culture.
